text:
Git branching is a powerful feature in version control systems that helps teams manage code changes smoothly. Instead of working directly on the main codebase, developers create feature branches to work on new features or bug fixes independently. This keeps the main branch stable and prevents unfinished code from causing problems.

Once a developer completes their work on a branch, they create a pull request to propose merging their changes into the main branch. Other team members review the code for errors and suggest improvements before approving it. Finally, merging combines the branch's changes with the main line of development. This structured version control workflow reduces conflicts and makes collaboration more efficient.
